question_title: Will any political party or coalition acquire a supermajority in the 2026 Hungarian parliamentary elections?
summary: The 2026 Hungarian parliamentary elections on April 12 feature a genuinely competitive race for the first time under the post-2011 electoral system. Independent polls show Tisza leading Fidesz by ~10 points, while Fidesz-friendly polls show Fidesz ahead. All expert analyses and seat projections converge on the conclusion that a supermajority (133+ seats) is very unlikely for either party. Tisza is projected at 99-132 seats, Fidesz at 89-117. The electoral system CAN produce supermajorities (it did so 4 consecutive times), but requires one dominant party with limited competition — the presence of Tisza as a strong challenger plus Mi Hazánk at 5-7% fragments the vote. The only plausible supermajority path is Fidesz winning (requires independent polls to be catastrophically wrong), but even Fidesz-optimistic projections don't clearly reach 133.

factors.description: Expert analysis (Grayling, Speyside) converges that supermajority for either party is low-probability; neither projected near 133 seats
Seat projections from all models show gap to 133: Tisza at 99-132, Fidesz at 89-117
Two competitive major parties plus Mi Hazánk (5-7%) fragment the vote, making supermajority much harder than in previous elections with weak/fragmented opposition
Electoral system structurally can produce supermajorities from 45-50% vote share (4 consecutive precedents under current rules)

Major polling disagreement: independent polls show Tisza +10, Fidesz-friendly polls show Fidesz ahead. If Fidesz-friendly polls correct and independent polls wildly wrong, Fidesz supermajority becomes possible
7 weeks until election — enough time for campaign dynamics, Fidesz state machine mobilization, or Tisza misstep to shift the race
Fidesz has outperformed polls in every election under new system; structural advantages (media control, gerrymandering, out-of-country votes) systematically underweighted by independent polls

reasoning:
**Forecast: 9% probability of any party winning a supermajority in the 2026 Hungarian elections.**

Key reasoning:
- This is the first genuinely competitive election under Hungary's post-2011 electoral system, with Tisza emerging as a formidable challenger to Fidesz
- All seat projections show neither party near the 133-seat supermajority threshold (Tisza projected at 99-132 seats, Fidesz at 89-117)
- Expert analyses unanimously describe supermajority as a "low-probability outcome"
- The electoral system CAN mechanically produce supermajorities (it did so 4 consecutive times), but this required weak/fragmented opposition — conditions that no longer hold
- The only realistic supermajority scenario is Fidesz, which would require independent polls to be massively wrong (~6-8% contribution to total). A Tisza supermajority is near-impossible given the system's structural advantages for the incumbent (~1%)

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as Yes if any party or coalition acquires a ⅔ supermajority (i.e., at least 133 seats) in the first Hungarian parliamentary elections held after this question opens, according to official election results.
fine_print:
If no parliamentary elections take place in Hungary before January 1, 2027, this question will be annulled.
